commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: A maintainer should review and accept the compatibility/availability tradeoff; there is no narrow automated repair to apply after the latest configured-timeout fix.; Cleared: The diff touches CLI runtime, docs, and tests only; I found no dependency, workflow, lockfile, secret-handling, or supply-chain change. (sha=6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds CLI-level hard timeout handling for local `openclaw agent` and embedded fallback runs, updates the agent CLI docs, and adds focused timeout, signal, and exit behavior tests.

PR surface: Source +276, Tests +384, Docs 0. Total +660 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. with source-level confidence: current main awaits embedded `agentCommand` for local/fallback paths without a command-level hard boundary, and the PR body includes before/after terminal output for the timeout symptom. I did not rerun the live local-agent scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer should review and accept the compatibility/availability tradeoff; there is no narrow automated repair to apply after the latest configured-timeout fix.

target.title: fix(cli): enforce local agent hard timeout
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw agent --local --timeout N` can still leave the CLI alive until an outer wrapper timeout kills it. The lower-level embedded agent timeout is useful, but it is not a command-level wall-clock guarantee: setup, plugin registration, retries, cleanup, or a wedged embedded run can keep the CLI process around after the operator-specified timeout window.

## Change and value

This PR adds a CLI-level hard timeout for embedded local agent runs. Explicit `--local` runs and embedded fallback runs now share a wall-clock boundary of the resolved finite timeout plus 30 seconds of grace. If that timer fires, the CLI aborts the local run signal and exits with code `124`, matching the exit code operators already see from GNU `timeout` wrappers.

The value is that scripts no longer need to wait for their own much larger timeout window just because the embedded run failed to settle cleanly.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and automation running `openclaw agent --local` with a finite `--timeout` or configured agent timeout.
- Affected: gateway-backed runs only after they enter embedded fallback; the normal Gateway request path keeps its existing timeout and `chat.abort` behavior.
- Not affected: `--timeout 0` or a configured local agent timeout of `0`; those continue to mean no CLI hard timeout.
- Not fixed here: post-success event-loop handles that keep the process alive after a successful local result. That is covered separately by #86264.

## Why now

Local diagnostics showed the no-completion CLI-exit class directly: an `agent --local` run can fail to produce a final result before the outer wrapper kills it, even when the inner timeout is shorter. This patch makes the local CLI boundary own that wall-clock guarantee instead of relying only on the embedded runner to unwind itself.

## Implementation

- Arms a startup local-agent hard timer before full plugin/command registration, so `agent --local --timeout N` is bounded even if setup hangs before the agent command action is reached.
- When `--timeout` is omitted, resolves the startup boundary from `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` before command/plugin registration instead of using an unconditional fallback; explicit `--timeout` still wins, and `--timeout 0` disables the timer.
- Keeps the lower-level embedded-run helper boundary around the existing `agentCommand` call sites.
- Uses the resolved finite timeout plus 30s as the hard deadline, with `0` disabling the timer.
- Forwards the resolved timeout into `agentCommand`, so cooperative embedded abort and the outer hard boundary agree on the same timeout.
- Preserves `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` precedence: signals still exit promptly with `130`/`143`, even if the embedded run ignores abort or rejects during teardown.
- On timeout, aborts the signal, writes a concise timeout error, and exits through `runtime.exit(124)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent --local --timeout N` and embedded fallback runs should have a command-level wall-clock timeout even if setup or the local run fails to settle after lower-level aborts.

Real environment tested: Linux/WSL local OpenClaw setup. Before baseline was global OpenClaw `2026.5.20 (e510042)` with the local `cli-exit-fix` patch still applied. Current PR head is `6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4`. The original live timeout proof was captured on #86276 head `e0e10b389dc3c08b27890db5d78e47d3fc9ef58f`; the follow-up configured-timeout correction on `6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4` is covered by the focused regression tests listed in Verification. Combined stack proof used #86276 `e0e10b389dc3c08b27890db5d78e47d3fc9ef58f` plus #86264 `98802c0f63c0a4fcc8639b8dde535f3bed879207`, combined head `6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
timeout 70s node /tmp/openclaw-combined-package/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent eval-1 --timeout 1 --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1 -m 'Reply with exactly the word: PONG' --json
timeout 300s node /tmp/openclaw-cli-exit-combined/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent eval-1 --timeout 180 --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1 -m 'Reply with exactly the word: PONG' --json
timeout 240s node /tmp/openclaw-cli-exit-combined/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id combined-cli-exit-proof --message 'Return marker OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK' --thinking off --json
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal transcripts and copied console output were captured under `/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/2026-05-25-cli-exit-combined/`, including `25-after-combined-package-early-boundary-timeout1.out`, `31-after-combined-source-success-pong.out`, and `32-after-combined-source-mock-success.out`.

```text
local agent command timed out after 1s plus 30s grace
EXIT_CODE=124
ELAPSED_SEC=39

[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] ... phase=attempt-dispatch ...
local agent command timed out after 180s plus 30s grace
EXIT_CODE=124
ELAPSED_SEC=212

finalAssistantVisibleText: OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK
executionTrace.runner: embedded
executionTrace.attempts[0].result: success
mock request path: /v1/responses
EXIT_CODE=0
ELAPSED_SEC=41
ASSERT_AGENT_TURN=passed
```

Observed result after fix: The `--timeout 1` local-agent command exited from OpenClaw's own hard boundary with code `124` after the configured timeout plus grace, instead of falling through to the outer wrapper. A longer live local-agent run also exited from OpenClaw's own `180s + 30s` boundary. The combined success-path run produced a final embedded-agent result and exited normally with code `0`.

Before evidence: On the production baseline, the local workaround was still firing instead of upstream owning the timeout.

```bash
timeout 70s openclaw agent --local --agent eval-1 --timeout 1 --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1 -m 'Reply with exactly the word: PONG' --json
```

```text
[openclaw] cli-exit-fix: hard wall-clock SIGKILL after 31s (timeout=1s + 30s grace)
EXIT_CODE=1
ELAPSED_SEC=40
```

Before evidence optional: During review, old draft #86276 head `8790ec43051f30fb48a5b8772ff4f3671f9307cd2` stacked with #86264 still exited only from an outer `timeout 70s` wrapper and did not print OpenClaw's timeout message. A plugin trace showed repeated pre-agent discovery/registration work, proving the first draft armed the boundary too late.

What was not tested: a standalone package install from this exact branch without #86264. The success-path exit proof above is intentionally run on the combined stack because #86264 owns the post-completion cleanup class.

## Verification

Standalone #86276 head `6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4`:

- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 600s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cli/run-main.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (41/41; covers configured omitted-timeout resolver, explicit override precedence, and configured `0` disabling)
- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 600s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (90/90; covers omitted `--timeout` plus configured timeout before command registration)
- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 600s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (54/54)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

Combined stack `6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680`:

- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test:unit:fast -- src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

## Relationship to #86264

#86264 handles the post-completion cleanup class: a command has finished but deferred context-engine maintenance keeps work alive. This PR handles the no-completion class: a local/embedded agent command never settles, so the CLI needs a hard wall-clock boundary. The combined mock-agent proof verifies the two fixes compose: successful local results still exit normally, while wedged/no-completion runs are capped by this PR.

AI-assisted: yes.
target.author: kaspre

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
